summary: Resend email integration (replacing TransIP SMTP) tested end-to-end: 16/16 pytest cases pass. Verified (a) /api/inbox/compose to droomvriendje12@gmail.com returns 200 with real Resend email id and persisted sent doc, (b) compose to random@example.com returns 502 with clear Resend test-mode error (no SMTP/SSL leakage), (c) /api/inbox/{id}/reply preserves In-Reply-To and References threading headers, (d) GET /api/inbox/folders, /api/inbox, /api/inbox/{id} regress green, (e) inbox webhook ingest still works, (f) /api/admin/orders/{id}/tracking with send_email=true returns 200 and routes through Resend (test-mode warn for non-verified customer is logged-only, no 500), (g) /api/admin/orders and /api/admin/customers regress green, (h) services.email_sender.send_email returns success=True for verified and success=False (no exception) for unverified, (i) smtplib.SMTP_SSL is NEVER invoked when sending via Resend (monkeypatched spy never tripped).

minor.issue: When SENDER_EMAIL=onboarding@resend.dev (test mode) AND TEST_RECIPIENT is set, the helper does NOT short-circuit unverified recipients client-side — every send attempt counts against the Resend quota even though it will be rejected. Consider checking `if SENDER_EMAIL=='onboarding@resend.dev' and to_email != TEST_RECIPIENT: return success=False locally` to save quota during the test-mode window.
Function name '_send_smtp' is misleading now that it delegates to Resend. Rename to _send_email for clarity (purely cosmetic).
`import smtplib` is still present at top of server.py but no longer used by send_email shim. Safe to remove for cleanliness, but kept for any legacy code paths. Not a functional issue.

Test-mode rejection currently bubbles up as 502 with the raw Resend SDK message. UI-friendly translation (e.g., 'Verzending alleen toegestaan naar geverifieerd test-adres') would help; current copy is English-only.

action_items: (Optional) Add domain droomvriendjes.com at https://resend.com/domains so non-test recipients can receive emails. Until then, ALL non-droomvriendje12@gmail.com sends will return 502 (compose) or be silently dropped with a warning (transactional flows like delivered/tracking).
(Optional) Short-circuit unverified recipients in services/email_sender.py when running in onboarding@resend.dev sender mode to save Resend quota.
(Optional) Rename routes/inbox.py::_send_smtp -> _send_email; remove unused `import smtplib` from server.py top.
(Optional) Localise the 502 error detail in routes/inbox.py to Dutch for UI consistency.
critical_code_review_comments: services/email_sender.py is clean, well-documented and correctly threads In-Reply-To / References. Good use of asyncio.to_thread for the sync SDK.
server.py send_email shim correctly delegates to Resend and preserves the boolean return contract used by csv_import_route and campaign sender — no API surface change.
routes/inbox.py compose/reply now correctly surface Resend failures as 502 (not 500), so the admin UI can render the test-mode error.
smtplib import remains at server.py:18 but is functionally unused — recommend removing for clarity (low priority).
server.py still 4113 lines; previous reviews recommend modularisation. Email migration is unrelated to that issue.
test_credentials: admin / Droomvriendjes2024! via POST /api/admin/login. Verified Resend recipient: droomvriendje12@gmail.com.
seed_data_creation: Inbox seeded one TEST_resend_reply message via /api/inbox/dev/ingest-raw; produced 2 successful Resend sends (verified recipient) + 1 test-mode rejection + 1 reply. Total Resend quota used this run: ~3 successful sends, well under daily test-mode budget.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Resend integration is green. Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_resend_email.py (16 tests). Backend logs confirm '✅ EMAIL SENT via Resend' for verified recipient and '⚠️ Resend test-mode restriction' for unverified — no SMTP/SSL/TransIP errors anywhere in the call path. Tracking POST currently triggers Resend send_review_request which is logged-only on test-mode rejection (does not 500). When user verifies droomvriendjes.com domain in Resend, change SENDER_EMAIL in /app/backend/.env to noreply@droomvriendjes.com (or similar) and re-run this suite — the 'unverified -> 502' test will then need updating (it would succeed).
rca of the issue: N/A — this iteration is a migration validation, not a bug fix. All 16 tests pass against the live deployment URL https://mollie-payments-test.preview.emergentagent.com. Two initial test failures were test-only issues (pytest didn't load backend/.env into os.environ → fixed by loading env into os.environ; tracking endpoint uses field name 'tracking_code'/'carrier' not 'tracking_number'/'tracking_carrier' → fixed in test). No product code changes were needed.
